---
title: "Day 17 — Words, tokens, and why models are bad at spelling"
subtitle: "Sunday, 9 August 2026 · Week 3 · Tokens and attention · Study day · 3-4 h"
---

::: {.day-nav .kind-study}
[← Day 16](day-16.qmd) · [All 50 days](../timeline.qmd) · [Day 18 →](day-18.qmd)
:::

::: {.why}
Arithmetic that fails on long numbers, code that changes meaning when you reindent it, non-English prompts costing three times as much per sentence - these trace back to a design decision made before the model has a single parameter. Today is the theory of that decision. Tomorrow you implement it.
:::

## What you should be able to do tonight

- You can run BPE by hand on a short corpus and produce the merge list in the right order.
- You can state BPE's merge criterion and WordPiece's in one line of arithmetic each, and say why the second one prefers different pairs.
- You can predict, before running the tokenizer, which of your ten field-specific strings cl100k_base will shred into the most pieces, and be right about why.

## Tasks <span class='task-total'>205 min</span>

::: {.task-list}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [75 min]{.task-min} Read SLP3 Ch2 up to and through the byte pair encoding section. Do not read the BPE algorithm passively - stop and run it by hand on a five-word toy corpus, writing each merge and the vocabulary after it.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [45 min]{.task-min} Read the rest of Ch2: text normalization and case folding, the WordPiece and unigram/SentencePiece alternatives, word normalization and lemmatization, sentence segmentation. Note in one line each what SentencePiece does differently that makes it work on languages without spaces.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [60 min]{.task-min} Tokenize your ten strings with tiktoken's cl100k_base and record the token count and the actual pieces for each. Then add these and explain every surprise: '2026' versus '20260', ' colocalization' with a leading space versus without, four spaces of indentation, and the same clinical sentence in English and in one other language.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [25 min]{.task-min} Write a short note listing the model behaviours tokenization explains - digit grouping and arithmetic errors, whitespace sensitivity in code, rare gene and drug names fragmenting into five or six pieces, the cost multiplier on non-English text. One sentence each, tied to a specific example from your own table.
:::
:::

## Resources

- [SLP3 Ch2 - Words and Tokens](https://web.stanford.edu/~jurafsky/slp3/2.pdf) [textbook]{.res-kind}
- [tiktoken](https://github.com/openai/tiktoken) [repo]{.res-kind}
- [Hugging Face - Summary of the tokenizers](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/tokenizer_summary) [docs]{.res-kind}

## Deliverable

::: {.deliverable}
A note holding your hand-run BPE merges on the toy corpus, and a table of cl100k_base token counts and splits for your ten field-specific strings plus the four probe cases, with one line each on why the split came out that way.
:::

## Checkpoint

::: {.checkpoint}
Corpus: low low lower lowest. Three merges. Write the merges in order and the final vocabulary. Then say what changes if the selection criterion is WordPiece's count(ab)/(count(a) x count(b)) instead of raw pair frequency, and which pair each criterion picks first.
:::

## Prepare for tomorrow

Ten minutes tonight so Day 18, Monday, 10 August 2026 starts clean. This is what the evening email sends you.

::: {.task-list .prep}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Register for an NCBI API key from your NCBI account settings - it raises the E-utilities rate limit from 3 to 10 requests per second, and you will want it tomorrow.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Paste one esearch URL for db=pubmed into your browser and confirm it returns XML with a list of PMIDs.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Clone minbpe and read exercise.md - just the four step headings, not the detail.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Decide your PubMed query now (a disease or exposure you know well) and write it down, because you will build the corpus on it tomorrow and still be using it on Day 33.
:::
:::
